---
title: "Home"
about:
  template: jolla
  image: images/ajay_4most.jpg
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:ajay[.dev@research.uwa.edu.au](mailto:.dev@research.uwa.edu.au)
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/ajdev-astro
    #- icon: google
    #  text: Google Scholar
    #  href: YOUR_GOOGLE_SCHOLAR_LINK
    - icon: person-badge
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1655-8178
---


# Ajay Dev

**Astrophysicist | Galaxy evolution | Galaxy--halo connection | Baryons in dark matter haloes**

I am an astrophysicist at the International Centre for Radio Astronomy Research, University of Western Australia. My research focuses on galaxy formation and evolution, the galaxy--halo connection, and the baryonic content of dark matter haloes.

I use multi-wavelength observations to study how stars, neutral hydrogen, and hot X-ray gas populate galaxies and galaxy groups. My work combines large extragalactic surveys including GAMA, ASKAP/DINGO, ASKAP/WALLABY, eROSITA, DEVILS, 4MOST/WAVES, and 4MOST/4HS.

## Research interests

* Galaxy formation and evolution
* Galaxy--halo connection
* Baryon census of dark matter haloes
* Multi-phase gas in galaxies and groups
* HI and X-ray stacking
* Large extragalactic surveys
* Feedback and gas accretion in galaxies
* AGN--galaxy coevolution

## Current focus

My current research aims to map the cold gas, hot gas, and stellar content of dark matter haloes across halo mass, environment, and cosmic time. This includes measuring the HI--halo mass relation using spectral stacking and combining radio, optical, and X-ray measurements to build a multi-wavelength census of baryons in the Universe.
